svg.text: LIBRAS
CHEROLOGY — SOUTH AMERICAN SIGN PARAMETERS
LIBRAS HANDSHAPES
LIBRAS: broad range of handshapes; some shared with LSF/ASL (LSF ancestor)
Iconic handshapes common: many LIBRAS signs visually motivated
Some handshapes initialized from Portuguese letters
LIBRAS LOCATION AND BODY SPACE
LIBRAS: rich use of body space; many signs on torso/arms
Neutral space: in front of signer; body contact: specific meaning
LIBRAS NMM: facial expression + mouth patterns in Portuguese
LIBRAS REGIONAL VARIATION
Brazil is vast: significant LIBRAS regional variation (North vs South)
Rio de Janeiro vs Sao Paulo: different signs for common words
INES (National Institute of Deaf Education): works toward standardization
LSA CHEROLOGY · SECONDARY LANGUAGE
LSA PARAMETERS
LSA: same five parameters as all sign languages
LSA handshapes: some overlap with LIBRAS (shared LSF heritage)
But most content signs: different from LIBRAS (distinct languages)
LSA NMM AND MOUTH PATTERNS
LSA: Spanish mouthing common with signs
Eyebrow grammar: same as all sign languages (yes/no vs wh-questions)
Gaze: important in LSA spatial grammar
SHARED SOUTH AMERICAN FEATURES
Both LIBRAS and LSA: five-parameter sign languages (universal)
Both: signed iconicity common; many visually motivated signs
Both: mouthing from national spoken language (Portuguese / Spanish)
Despite geographic proximity: LIBRAS and LSA are distinct, not mutually intelligible
PLATE 66b ALT · LIBRAS / LSA · CHEROLOGY
